title: Vaccines
puzzle: How can a shot prepare your body for an illness you have not had yet?
insight: Vaccines safely expose the immune system to a version or fragment of a threat so the body can practice recognizing it before real exposure.
key_ideas: They help the body react faster later.
Widespread vaccination can reduce spread through communities.
mind_blower: A vaccine is like a rehearsal that changes the ending of a future encounter.
next_curiosity: If vaccines train immune memory, why do some of them need boosters?
narration_text: How can a shot prepare your body for an illness you have not had yet? Vaccines safely expose the immune system to a version or fragment of a threat so the body can practice recognizing it before real exposure. Vaccines train immune memory. They help the body react faster later. Widespread vaccination can reduce spread through communities. A vaccine is like a rehearsal that changes the ending of a future encounter. If vaccines train immune memory, why do some of them need boosters?
